    damage that comes along with being publicly shamed for what may be relatively minor infractions’.
    See Ohlberg, Ahmed and Lang (n 23) 11

    An example is the challenge of collaborative research and data sharing between the EU and China
    under the GDPR and the Chinese data protection regime. See van Deursen and Kummeling (n 67).
                                                  TRANSNATIONAL LEGAL THEORY            309

conception of Jessup, the CSCS is a ‘transnational legal problem’ as it spans
across corporate, geopolitical as well as individual spheres. It does not only
determine legality and illegality of a behaviour but in addition uses more
ﬂuid means to assess and inﬂuence behaviour and shapes the transnational
construction and ﬂow of norms. Hence, the CSCS could mark a new
chapter in the global regulatory contest over corporate governance and the
international economic order.
